message:
feat(pool): add AsyncConnectionManager and AsyncDbPool for async database backends

Add a full async connection pool implementation alongside the existing
synchronous DbPool. AsyncConnectionManager is the async counterpart of
ConnectionManager, designed for backends like PostgreSQL whose connect
and validate operations are async and return Outcome via a Cx.

AsyncDbPool provides the same lifecycle management (idle eviction, max
age, validate-on-checkout, close/drain) as DbPool but with async get()
taking a Cx for cancellation. AsyncPooledConnection auto-returns to the
pool on drop with explicit return_to_pool() and discard() methods.

Also update quic_h3_e2e_violations test assertions: revert error message
for write-after-close and open-after-close back to "connection is closed"
(the correct message for closed connections, as distinct from the "1-RTT
traffic not yet enabled" message used in drain state).

Refresh raptorq wavefront benchmark artifact with latest timing results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
